mnemonics.title: Initial p Value
mnemonics.content: P₀ = 1 - r: Remember as 'One Minus Radius' - just subtract r from 1.

mnemonics.title: Decision Rule
mnemonics.content: When in doubt (pk < 0), go East. When sure (pk ≥ 0), go South-East. Think: 'Neg = East, NonNeg = SE'.

mnemonics.title: Stop Condition
mnemonics.content: Stop when x > y: 'x crosses y' - think of diagonal line at 45°.

mnemonics.title: 8-way Symmetry
mnemonics.content: Calculate 1/8, reflect 8 times: Like an octagon mirror - 1 face shows all.

acronyms.usage: Pixel at (x+1, y) position - 'E'ast means moving right
Pixel at (x+1, y-1) position - 'S'outheast means down-right
Determines which pixel to choose: p < 0 → E, p ≥ 0 → SE
Distance from center to any point on circle

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a straight line between two candidate pixels E and SE. The midpoint is exactly halfway. If the circle passes above this midpoint, choose E; if below or through, choose SE.
Picture a circle divided by 8 pizza slices. Draw points in one slice (45° wedge), then mirror them to all 7 other slices.
Think of p as a 'push' - if negative, the circle 'pushes' you east; if positive/zero, you go southeast.
Start at top (0,r), walk clockwise staying on circle boundary. Each step either goes right (E) or down-right (SE) to stay as close as possible.

analogies.analogy: Like walking around a circular track. You can step straight ahead (East) or diagonal (South-East). You always choose whichever keeps you closest to the track's edge.
Like drawing one spoke of a wheel and then copying it to all 8 positions around the center.
Using whole steps on a grid instead of precise angles - like moving in chess moves (1 square at a time) rather than measuring exact angles.
Like a compass that tells you which direction keeps you closest to the circle - negative means circle is 'to your right', non-negative means 'below or through'.
